hypothesis: Pretraining X‑MoPA with an additional single‑to‑one latent prediction objective—where during pretraining the model is randomly given only one modality and must predict a second modality in latent space—will produce representations that, after fine‑tuning, yield lower MAE on MatBench regression tasks and higher accuracy under single‑modality missing or corrupted conditions (e.g., missing XRD or text) compared to the original two‑to‑one pretraining scheme.
